input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.base_prompt: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

visible_c_inputs.question: In which year was the album released which included the single "Long Kiss Goodnight", which was used as a sample in the song "Rap name"?
passages: Rap Name | "Rap Name" is the debut single of American rapper Obie Trice, released as a limited edition vinyl recording, taken from the deluxe edition of the soundtrack to the film "8 Mile".  The song features vocals from fellow-label mate Eminem, who sampled the track in two tracks for his album "The Eminem Show", released earlier in 2002.  These tracks are "Drips" and the intro of the album's lead single, "Without Me", as well as a few other lines from the song.  The song also features a vocal sample of The Notorious B.I.G. from his song "Long Kiss Goodnight" from the album "Life After Death".  The official video was filmed in Detroit with featured cameo appearances by Eminem's group D12.

Church (song) | "Church" is a song by rapper T-Pain.  It is a fast-paced song led by guitar riffs and is one of the songs in which T-Pain raps.  It appears on his album "Epiphany" and was released as the third single.  The song "features" Teddy Verseti, one of T-Pain's aliases, which he uses when rapping vigorously.  The song is about wanting to fight someone following a confrontation at a club.  It was confirmed as the third single from "Epiphany" in an interview promoting the album.  It contains a sample of the song "Which Way to America" by Living Colour.

The Game (Alyssa Reid song) | "The Game" is a song by "The Next Star" contestant Alyssa Reid from her 2011 album "The Game".  It is the title track of the album and the album's second single, as well as Alyssa's second most famous single, beat out by her previous single "Alone Again", officially released May 31st, 2011.  It was preceded by the album's lead single, "Alone Again", featuring P. Reign, which heavily sampled the 1987 Heart song "Alone", except included new verses and rap.  "The Game" samples parts of the new verses of "Alone Again".

Goodnight Kisses | "Goodnight Kisses" is a song by American R&B singer Charlie Wilson.  It was released on October 7, 2014.  The song is the first single from the seventh studio album "Forever Charlie" (2015).

Sons of the P | Sons of the P is the second album by American rap group Digital Underground, released on October 15, 1991.  The album featured two hit singles, "No Nose Job" and "Kiss You Back", both of which were written by, and featured the lead vocals of Greg Jacobs (a.k.a. Shock G/Humpty Hump), and the latter of which featured multi-layered choruses and background vocals sung by Boni Boyer, who briefly worked with D.U. shortly after her stint with Prince's Sign of the Times/Love Sexy band.  It is sometimes mistakenly reported that Kiss You Back was co-written & co-performed by George Clinton, but his name appropriately appears in the writers credit due to a sample of "(Not Just) Knee Deep" by Funkadelic.  He did however actively participate in the writing and recording of the title track "Sons of the P", to which he also contributed vocals, and which marked one of the earliest studio guest appearances by Clinton on a Hip Hop release, preceded only by Kurtis Blow's "Magilla Gorilla" released in 1986.  Both the album and the single "Kiss You Back" were each certified Gold by the RIAA.

Fatherhood (album) | Fatherhood was the third of five albums of home-made recordings, released by Stephen Jones under the name Baby Bird in 1995.  The album includes a version of the song "Goodnight", which was subsequently released as the first single by Jones' band Babybird (written as one word).

Kiss You Back | "Kiss You Back" was the lead single released from Digital Underground's second studio album (their third official release), "Sons of the P".  The song sampled Funkadelic's 1979 hit, "(Not Just) Knee Deep" for which the song's writers, George Clinton and Philippé Wynne, received writing credits.

c_plus_state.generated_query: Which album included the single "Long Kiss Goodnight" (the single sampled in the song "Rap Name"), and in what year was that album released?
retrieved_titles: What Would You Do? (City High song)

w_minus_state.generated_query: "Alan Ruck birth date July 1, 1956 You Can Count on Me Ferris Bueller's Day Off"

w_plus_state.output: First-hop evidence: the Ferris Bueller's Day Off page (retrieved) lists the film's stars including Matthew Broderick, Mia Sara, and Alan Ruck. The Alan Ruck page in the first-hop results gives his birth date as July 1, 1956 (this is first-hop evidence only). Bridge needed: the actor who appears in both Ferris Bueller's Day Off and You Can Count on Me (i.e., the overlapping cast member). Retrieval direction for next hop: 1) fetch the You Can Count on Me page to obtain its cast list and identify which Ferris Bueller actor appears there; 2) fetch that identified actor's biography page (e.g., the Matthew Broderick page is already listed as a retrieval target) to obtain the actor's birth date. Do not assume which Ferris Bueller actor is the overlap without checking the You Can Count on Me cast list.
w_plus_state.generated_query:
1) Retrieve the You Can Count on Me (2000 film) page (cast section) and return the full credited cast list.
2) Retrieve the biography pages for Matthew Broderick, Mia Sara, and Alan Ruck and return each actor's birth date (date and place of birth as listed). 
3) Indicate which of those three actors (if any) appears in the You Can Count on Me cast and provide that actor's birth date.
retrieved_titles: List of Fawlty Towers cast members
The Register of the Victoria Cross
w_plus_state.summary_2: Matthew Broderick — the actor who appears in both You Can Count on Me and Ferris Bueller's Day Off — was born on March 21, 1962.

delta_p.trigger: Apply when agent_role == 'summary1' and the question links a person across two works (e.g., asks for a star who appears in both A and B) and the visible passages include a film page with a cast list and at least one actor biography.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep extracting and reporting concise factual first-hop facts already present in the passages (e.g., 'Alan Ruck — born July 1, 1956') and retain the current short factual style for such items.
delta_p.change_rule: Instead of returning a single final fact, produce a compact, structured summary with three labeled parts: (1) 'First-hop evidence:' name the retrieved passage(s) and the concrete facts they provide (very short phrases); (2) 'Bridge needed:' explicitly state the overlap that must be checked in the next hop (phrase it as a check to identify which Ferris Bueller cast member also appears in You Can Count on Me, without asserting which one); (3) 'Retrieval direction:' list ordered next retrieval steps (e.g., fetch the You Can Count on Me cast list; compare it to the Ferris Bueller cast; then fetch the matching actor's biography to obtain birth date). Keep each part concise (1–3 short clauses).
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not produce a final birth date or assert which actor is the overlap here; do not assume overlaps without checking the You Can Count on Me cast; avoid long verbatim spans from non-visible pages and avoid inventing facts.
delta_p.output_contract: Return the usual 'summary' output string. That string must include the three labeled short sections exactly: 'First-hop evidence:', 'Bridge needed:', and 'Retrieval direction:'. Each section should briefly list the relevant passage names/facts and the explicit next-hop retrieval actions (ordered). Do not include a final answer or unverified conclusions.
delta_p.why_local_transition: The current behavior returned a lone birth date from a first-hop actor page. For a multi-hop pipeline the summary must instead expose which overlap to check and give explicit, ordered retrieval steps so the next agent can find and verify the correct overlapping actor and then retrieve that actor's biography for the birth date.

match.transported_damage_hypothesis: Make the summary1 agent stop returning the final release year and instead output a compact 3-part summary: 'First-hop evidence:' (cite the song/single page facts that show the single exists), 'Bridge needed:' (explicitly say which album must be checked to see that it includes the single), and 'Retrieval direction:' (ordered next-hop steps: fetch that album page and extract the release year).
match.expected_damaged_behavior: For this C case, summary1 will not emit '1997' as the answer. Instead it will produce three labeled short sections (First-hop evidence, Bridge needed, Retrieval direction) that expose the missing bridge (which album contains the single) and instruct a follow-up retrieval of the album page to obtain the release year.
match.rationale: The W-139 repair enforces surfacing a bridge (an overlap between two works) and explicit next-hop retrieval steps rather than asserting a final fact. The C question likewise requires finding a bridge (which album contains the named single) before extracting the release year. The failure pattern (refusing to give the final numeric year and forcing a three-part first-hop summary + retrieval plan) transfers directly: it preserves local neighborhood (only alters summary1 behavior), doesn't reuse any W-specific entities, and produces damage that remains a local next-hop request for the album page rather than flipping the answer.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are constructing a target-specific transported damaged output C_minus
for one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Inputs:
- C_plus is a correct current output/state for the target sample.
- W_minus -> W_plus is a matched W-side repair transition.
- The ordered W delta-p trace describes the repair behavior.

Task:
Infer the reverse W failure pattern and apply only that failure pattern to
the target sample's own visible inputs, producing a plausible LOCAL neighbor
C_minus of C_plus.

Strict rules:
- Produce an actual output for the selected agent boundary, not a prompt.
- Preserve the agent's native input/output contract.
- Use only visible_c_inputs as factual content.
- Never copy W-side entities, answers, titles, or sample-specific literals
  unless they independently occur in visible_c_inputs.
- Do not create unrelated corruption. Preserve enough useful C context for
  the result to remain a local neighbor.
- Damage one coherent behavior: weaken, remove, or blur an anchor, relation,
  qualifier, candidate distinction, evidence integration, output selection,
  or another matched failure pattern.
- An answer flip is not required and must not be claimed.
- Do not mention gold labels, oracle targets, evaluation metrics, source or
  target states, transport, prompt updates, or optimization in damaged_output.
- Do not wrap damaged_output in explanatory prose.

summary1
